_comment: Per-role suggested-app bundles as winget package IDs. The ninite-personalized module reads which apps are already installed and emits one question per not-yet-installed suggestion (Yes / No / What's this?). NEVER auto-installs — module always writes a copy-paste command for the user to run explicitly.
_alwaysInclude: The 'recommended-modern' bundle is offered regardless of detected role. It captures the good-practice baseline that any modern user benefits from (7-Zip, PowerToys, Everything, VLC, password manager, code editor, terminal). Skip individual entries the user already has.
_wingetIdVerify: winget IDs occasionally change (publisher renames, new suffixes). If an install command fails with 'no package found', run `winget search <name>` at runtime to resolve the current ID.
_appSchema: Per-app fields: id (winget package ID), name (human), whyItMatters (one-sentence blurb shown in the ask), sizeMB (approx install size), publisher, category, commonAmong ('everyone' | 'developers' | 'creators' | 'gamers' | 'office workers'), optional (bool: skip in the fast recommendation pass), askOnlyIf (optional gate: 'local_media' = only ask if user answered 'local files' to the music/video usage runtime question).
_deliberatelyOmitted: Password managers (Bitwarden, 1Password, etc.) are DELIBERATELY not in this bundle. Choosing a password manager is a personal decision with real consequences and pc-cleaner should not push a specific one. If the user asks for a suggestion, list Bitwarden (free), 1Password (paid) and KeePassXC (fully offline) with one line each — but do not include them in the automatic recommendation pass.
_singleChoiceCategories: Browser: only Google Chrome is recommended (widely compatible, syncs). Image viewer: only IrfanView. If the user explicitly asks about alternatives (Firefox, Nomacs, etc.), list them, but the default recommendation is single-choice.

bundles.description: Good-practice baseline for any modern PC. Offer to every user regardless of role — skip individual apps they already have. Based on the seed machine's setup 2026-07-04.

apps.name: 7-Zip
apps.whyItMatters: Opens .zip / .rar / .7z / .tar.gz archives. Free. Windows' built-in archiver is worse in every way.

apps.name: Microsoft PowerToys
apps.whyItMatters: Microsoft-made pack of Windows tweaks: window snap, color picker, image resizer, run-anywhere launcher, and more.

apps.name: Everything
apps.whyItMatters: Instant filename search across your whole disk. Way faster than Windows Search — searches complete before you finish typing.

apps.name: VLC media player
apps.whyItMatters: Plays every video and audio format. Free.

apps.name: IrfanView
apps.whyItMatters: Fast image viewer with basic edits. Windows Photos is slow — this opens JPGs instantly.

apps.name: Audacity
apps.whyItMatters: Free audio editor. Trim voice memos, remove background noise, convert formats.

apps.name: HandBrake
apps.whyItMatters: Free video converter. Shrink big video files or convert formats.

apps.name: OBS Studio
apps.whyItMatters: Free screen recorder / streamer. Standard tool for tutorials and Twitch.

apps.name: Google Chrome
apps.whyItMatters: Fast, universally-supported browser. Syncs across devices with a Google account.

apps.name: Visual Studio Code
apps.whyItMatters: Free code editor most developers use. Great even for editing config files or notes.

apps.name: Windows Terminal
apps.whyItMatters: Modern replacement for the black Command Prompt. Tabs, colors, better fonts.

apps.name: Zoom
apps.whyItMatters: Video meetings. You'll need it as soon as someone sends a Zoom invite.

apps.name: Discord
apps.whyItMatters: Chat / voice / video app popular with gamers, hobby communities, and some workplaces.

apps.name: Slack
apps.whyItMatters: Work chat. Only needed if your workplace uses it.

apps.name: yt-dlp
apps.whyItMatters: Command-line downloader for YouTube and many other video sites. Free.

apps.name: Git
apps.whyItMatters: Version control. Almost every dev project uses it.

apps.name: GitHub CLI
apps.whyItMatters: Run GitHub actions (create PRs, review, merge) from the terminal.

apps.name: Python 3.12
apps.whyItMatters: Python runtime. Ubiquitous for scripts, data, AI.
apps.verify: Bump version pin to newest active branch periodically

apps.name: Node.js LTS
apps.whyItMatters: JavaScript runtime. Needed for anything front-end or npm-based.

apps.name: Docker Desktop
apps.whyItMatters: Run isolated Linux containers on Windows.

apps.name: Postman
apps.whyItMatters: GUI for testing HTTP APIs.

apps.name: JetBrains Toolbox
apps.whyItMatters: Installer / updater for JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm).

apps.name: PowerShell 7
apps.whyItMatters: Modern cross-platform PowerShell. Faster and better than the built-in one.

apps.name: Adobe Acrobat Reader
apps.whyItMatters: Reads PDFs. Free.

apps.name: GIMP
apps.whyItMatters: Free Photoshop-like image editor.

apps.name: Blender
apps.whyItMatters: Free professional 3D modeling / animation software.

apps.name: darktable
apps.whyItMatters: Free RAW photo editor (Lightroom alternative).
apps.verify: Publisher casing — sometimes shows as darktable.darktable

apps.name: Steam
apps.whyItMatters: The main PC game store / launcher.

apps.name: Epic Games Launcher
apps.whyItMatters: Epic's game store. Gives away free games weekly.

apps.name: GeForce Experience
apps.whyItMatters: NVIDIA driver updater + game overlay. Being replaced by NVIDIA App.
apps.verify: NVIDIA is moving toward the NVIDIA App as the successor

apps.name: MSI Afterburner
apps.whyItMatters: GPU monitoring and overclocking. Free.

apps.name: Microsoft Office
apps.whyItMatters: Word / Excel / PowerPoint desktop apps.
apps.verify: May be Microsoft.Office or Microsoft365.Apps depending on the account tier

apps.name: Notion
apps.whyItMatters: Notes + wiki + database + tasks in one app.

apps.name: Google Drive
apps.whyItMatters: Sync Google Drive files to your PC.

apps.name: Zotero
apps.whyItMatters: Reference / citation manager.

apps.name: Anki
apps.whyItMatters: Flashcard software using spaced repetition. Excellent for memorization.

apps.name: LibreOffice
apps.whyItMatters: Free Office suite. Reads .docx / .xlsx.
apps.verify: Publisher path — sometimes appears as LibreOffice.LibreOffice
